description: Consolidation decisions for widget_similarity.json clusters. decidedBy 'owner+claude' entries are taste decisions; execution status lives in codex_worklog.md.

decisions.rationale: Three ad-hoc label/value stat treatments; CatchStatColumn already covers all with monoValue flag. Standardizes stat typography (RunningStat flips from label-first kicker to value-first).
Title+subtitle section headers; CatchSectionHeader gained an optional subtitle slot. Call sites pass padding: EdgeInsets.zero to stay pixel-faithful.
Form-field wrapper (label + detail + child slot), not a stat; coarse fingerprint conflated it. Revisit against labelled.dart/CatchFormFieldLabel in a form-field pass.

Both re-implement Tooltip+CatchIconButton. Call sites pass size: CatchIconButton.defaultSize (canonical defaults navSize) and foregroundColor. Future candidate: rename CatchTopBarIconAction -> CatchIconAction since it is now used off top bars.

members.name: HostManageMetaItem
members.note: color: maps to icon tint; label defaults ink2

members.name: StageSectionLabel
members.note: icon section title, not meta; candidate for CatchSectionHeader icon variant later

decisions.rationale: One standard icon+label meta treatment (sm icon, labelM ink2, single line) added to catch_meta_row.dart beside CatchMetaDotRow.

members.name: CatchEventThumbnailScrimOverlay
members.note: already a parameterized core primitive tied to thumbnail styles; may delegate to CatchScrim later

decisions.rationale: Four gradient recipes on one skeleton; centralizing them makes the photo-treatment language a design-system asset and standardizes IgnorePointer wrapping. WO-016 added CatchOpacity.photoFrameEdge so the photo-frame preset no longer borrows the Event Success border token.
All six are thin Center(child: CatchEmptyState(...)) content bindings around the existing primitive. Not components; catalogue noise. Inline at single call sites and delete.

Already consolidated internally: both delegate to shared _CatchErrorSpec + CatchErrorBody. Two named entry points (full-screen vs inline) are intentional API ergonomics; merging is churn without UI improvement.

members.name: CatchPersonRosterLayout
members.note: fix raw `size: 11` -> CatchIcon.micro (token drift vs chat layout)

decisions.rationale: Intentional presentation variants over shared CatchPersonRowData (chat preview with unread emphasis vs roster meta). Merging with flags would be a conditional mess. Real finding is the raw icon size literal.
Byte-identical fractional bar-fill across host/user analytics. Note the trend: MetricTile (0.73) and panel pairs suggest a full host/user analytics-kit dedupe pass later.
Byte-identical 'Who's going' skeleton duplicated across two files of the same feature.

Five compositions of the same 'surface + N (leading shape, text lines) rows [+ title]' pattern across three features. One core primitive with a leading-shape enum; standardized text-width jitter (exact per-row widths are skeleton noise). WO-016 added divided-row support and absorbed the two previously skipped host wrappers.
Both are 'row of N equal Expanded skeleton boxes with gap' (quick actions, tab pickers). Generic enough for the catch_skeleton.dart convenience family.

members.name: GenderFilterSkeleton
members.note: height param stays: chips it stands in for are taller

decisions.rationale: Same jittered chip-wrap skeleton with drifted heights (s8 vs s9) and meaningless width jitter differences; standardize jitter, parameterize height.

Same titled-surface + text-block + trailing-boxes skeleton, both event_success. Stays feature-level (single-feature composition). Codex must diff bodies below the shared prefix and escalate if not isomorphic.

ChatShareCardSheet hand-rolls capture/grabber/share-button that CatchShareCardSheet already provides (club/event share flows use it in production). Parameter mapping is one-to-one; chat keeps its card widget.

decisions.clusterId: group/rotation family (c016, c028, c029, c030, c033, c044, c052 + VisibleGroupRotationSlots pairs)
decisions.rationale: Domain fork, not UI drift: micro-pods vs guided-rotations carry parallel typed rounds (EventSuccessGroupOverrideRound vs RotationOverrideRound) and genuinely different business logic (opt-out semantics, intervals, fairness totals). Generic unification would trade duplication for conditional complexity. Both compose tokenized primitives already. Rule of three: revisit if a third assignment mode appears.

members.name: CatchTabDockIcon
members.note: rewrite to delegate its badge overlay to CatchCountBadge

decisions.rationale: CatchTabDockIcon copy-pastes the entire count-badge overlay (same layout tokens, same 99+ logic). Promote the overlay as CatchCountBadge(count, child); tab dock icon becomes a thin composition.

members.name: SavedEventsMessage
members.note: inline; unify overrides to CalendarMessage's set (fixes eventInfoTileExtent icon-size token misuse)

decisions.rationale: Thin CatchEmptyState wrappers (same policy as c009). CalendarMessage/SavedEventsMessage additionally drifted on typography overrides; standardize both surfaces on the calendar treatment. Recon task: count the CatchScreenBody+Center(CatchEmptyState) pattern repo-wide — if >=4, next batch designs a CatchEmptyState screen variant.

members.name: PhotoSlotMainBadge
members.note: into existing CatchBadge via uppercase + ink/inverse styling

decisions.rationale: Different concepts sharing a skeleton: MapPill is a translucent mono overlay pill for imagery (keep; fix raw alpha literal), PhotoSlotMainBadge is a status badge CatchBadge already covers.

members.name: CatchConfirmDialog
members.note: rewrite internals to delegate shell to CatchFormDialog if shells are copy-pasted

decisions.rationale: Two entry points (confirm vs form) are good API ergonomics, same as the error-state pair. The overlay shell (Dialog + CatchSurface + title + actions row) must be shared internally, not duplicated — verify and delegate.
Identical PreferredSizeWidget shells (48-height padded CatchOptionGroup) differing only in option enum. Generic core primitive; tokenizes the raw 48 as CatchLayout.tabRailHeight.

members.name: HostProfileMissingState
members.note: keeps its ListView wrapper at call site

decisions.rationale: Same 'surface + sectionTitle + supporting body + CTA actions' empty card three times in hosts. Feature-level for now; promote to core if WO-008 recon shows the pattern is cross-feature.
Six-line shared shell around CatchInlineErrorState with clear intent-carrying names; merge value below churn threshold.

Per-tab skeleton compositions; parameterizing would move the same jitter to call sites. Real finding: raw title-width literals (148/190/170/150) instead of CatchLayout.skeletonText* tokens — drift fix in WO-013.
Composition relationships, not duplication: one member instantiates the other (screen wraps its body/content via builder). Fingerprint similarity is pass-through plumbing. Rule K1.

Same file, identical bodies except the media: argument (polaroid artwork vs photo overlay). Single widget; if call sites pick the variant from photo availability, fold that choice inside (mirroring ClubShareArtwork), else keep a media param.

members.name: PaperTicketSerial
members.note: rewrite build to compute strings then return PaperTicketDetail (accepts value maxLines 1->2)

decisions.rationale: Serial is Detail with computed content; delegate internally, both APIs stay.
Deliberate dual art direction (celebration cream overlay vs paper surface) on the known celebration-screen debt file; parameterizing four visual knobs for a 20-line loop is churn.
Typed domain rows in an internal policy lab screen; generics over preview-row models is complexity without user-facing value. Rule K2 (domain fork) on a lab surface.

members.name: LabHero
members.note: delegates shell; keeps its outer Padding/Center/ConstrainedBox

decisions.rationale: The accent->ink diagonal gradient hero recipe (gradient + transparent border + contentRelaxed) is copy-pasted across three event_success screens. Extract the shell as a shared feature widget; each hero keeps its content.

members.name: ProfileInlineMultiChoiceEntryEditor
members.note: deferred — generic classes, need type-aware read

members.name: ExploreRailLabel
members.note: deferred — possible overlap with CatchOptionGroup item rendering

decisions.rationale: Deferred, not decided: classes are generic (class X<T>) and need a proper read in the next batch.

members.name: UnsavedChangesPill
members.note: warning-dot status indicator, distinct concept and tokens

decisions.rationale: Two content variants of the same CTA leading in the same file; parameterize icon+label. Not promoted to core: CTA-leading is an event-detail concept with 2 uses.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.
Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge.

Current-registry id for the prior c006 owner decision: StageSectionLabel is an icon section title, not an icon+label meta row; folding it into CatchMetaRow would cross section-title and meta-row concepts.
Current-registry id for the prior small-widget owner decision: UnsavedChangesPill is a warning-dot status indicator with distinct tokens, not an event-detail CTA leading icon+label.

K5 territory: both share the Semantics/InkWell/DecoratedBox/ConstrainedBox shell, but ActivityTypeRow's body is ActivityDot + titleL label + titleL count with selected semantics while MoreActivityTypesRow's body is kicker '+ n MORE TYPES' + forward arrow expand affordance. REVIEW ANSWER (claude, 2026-07-03): keep-distinct — divergent semantics would need ~5 knobs to reproduce; churn over value (K4/K5).

Current-registry id for prior c005-layout decision: chat preview with unread emphasis and roster meta are intentional presentation variants over CatchPersonRowData; the only drift was the raw roster icon size fixed in WO-005.

Current-registry id for prior c006-dialog/WO-010 decision: confirm and form dialogs remain separate public entry points; full-body inspection rejected direct delegation because action layout and content-slot contracts differ.
Current-registry id for the WO-006 skipped members. WO-016 added CatchSkeletonRows.divided and absorbed both divider-separated host wrappers into the shared primitive.
K2 domain fork: micro-pods and guided rotations carry parallel typed business logic and already compose tokenized primitives; generic unification would add conditional complexity.
K2 domain fork: group rotation slots and rotation slots bind to different assignment families with different semantics; keep the public widgets split until a third assignment mode appears.
K2 domain fork: group override and rotation override sheets are backed by parallel override model families and different business rules, so a generic sheet would concentrate conditional logic.
K2 domain fork: the round editors operate on EventSuccessGroupOverrideRound versus RotationOverrideRound and differ in opt-out/fairness/interval behavior, not just row chrome.
Current-registry id for prior c021-tab-skeleton/WO-013 decision: per-tab skeleton compositions stay distinct; the actionable drift was raw title-width literals, already tokenized in WO-013.
K2 domain fork from the prior event-policy rows decision: typed cancellation/result row groups live in an internal policy lab and generics would add complexity without user-facing value.
K2 domain fork from the prior event-policy rows decision: cancellation and result preview rows bind to different policy preview models and should not be unified by generic conditionals.
Current-registry id for prior c045-hero/WO-014 decision: the shared gradient hero shell was extracted as EventSuccessHeroSurface and the public heroes now delegate while keeping their content APIs.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: dashboard home variants are screen composition, not duplicate primitives.
K4 churn threshold from prior c061-companion-error decision: six-line shared shell around CatchInlineErrorState is below the threshold, and the names carry distinct intent.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: group and reveal rotation slots carry different assignment semantics even though their row chrome is similar.
K4 churn threshold from prior c041-celebration-details-card decision: the cards intentionally carry different art direction, and parameterizing the visual knobs is churn.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: DashboardFull wraps DashboardFullSliverBody, so the similarity is pass-through composition rather than duplication.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: micro-pod and rotation schedule cards expose different assignment models and business logic.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: ProfileTab instantiates ProfileTabContent, so the match is wrapper/content composition.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: member and pair editors are backed by different override model families and should not be unified by conditional generics.

No exact K/R match: both are profile inline choice editors, but single-choice owns nullable selected state and optional empty-selection behavior while multi-choice owns Set<T> state, optional/non-optional removal rules, and patchForLatestProfile handling.

K5 concept mismatch: EventSharePill is a light share-card metadata pill using primarySoft/labelS/compactLabelContent; EventSuccessDarkPill is a dark editorial overlay pill using editorialLight alpha/labelL/compactControlContent.

No exact K/R match: ExploreRailLabel is structurally close to core CatchOptionGroupItem, but absorbing it requires a core option-item API decision around selectedRule, variant, option typing, and whether explore filter rails are option groups.

K5 concept mismatch: both render circular icon medallions, but CatchErrorIcon is the core danger/error medallion with configurable extent/iconSize while PaperCelebrationIcon is celebration-specific primary art direction with fixed 64/30 sizing.

K4 churn threshold: the mergeable shell is a small UserAnalyticsSection + CatchSurface + Column loop, but a shared panel would need label, row data, row builder, and separator policy knobs for two intent-carrying analytics concepts.

K5 concept mismatch: both are expandable disclosure shells, but CatchFrameworkErrorDebugDetails is core developer-error detail UI with danger label and debug text while SetupDisclosureSection is Event Success setup content with title, subtitle, and children.

K4 churn threshold: both use the same Event Detail surface/title/line spacing shell, but unifying them would require title, data derivation, conditional policy lines, and item-builder knobs for distinct overview concepts.
No exact K/R match: StructureNumberField was already kept as a form-field wrapper, while RevealHostCopy is event-success copy content; the shared skeleton does not identify one canonical component.
K5 concept mismatch: the shared icon/text/surface skeleton spans companion beats, live host cards, setup notices, and user analytics rows with different typography, token roles, and data contracts.

K4 churn threshold: the common code is the sliver padding/adapter/center/constrained shell, while the children are insights, skeleton, and profile content flows that would need child, spacing, and state knobs.
K5 concept mismatch: these icon/text rows and cards carry setup, dashboard, paper-companion, profile, safety, and swipe-window semantics with different label/body/kicker and surface roles.

K5 concept mismatch: all three are child surfaces, but StagePanel is the animated ambient stage card, StageActionDock is the dark action surface, and StageSoftBand is the soft nested band with different token roles.
K4 churn threshold: the overlap is generic skeleton surface/column/row scaffolding, but a shared widget would need chart, host summary, club host, and browse-grid knobs for distinct loading concepts.

K4 churn threshold: both are event-detail CTA cards in one file, but unifying them would require event/share versus companion/open behavior, title/body, and button mapping knobs for two intent-carrying concepts.
K4 churn threshold: the shared skeleton atoms are small row/column fragments, while reproducing all members would require count, axis, padding, row content, and feature-shell knobs.

K2 domain fork: the public app shell owns public navigation/guest auth affordances while HostAppShell owns host navigation; unifying would trade duplication for host/public conditional business logic.

K4 churn threshold: both render skeleton section surfaces, but the event-success primitive owns title/text/trailing counts while the profile surface skeleton owns profile-line count and surface padding.

No exact K/R match: the shared CatchSelectChip/Wrap shape hides different multi-select, questionnaire, reveal countdown, and rotation-cadence APIs that need a feature-level chip-group design decision.
K4 churn threshold: both use badge/wrap people tokens, but VisiblePodAssignment wraps assignment-state copy and shelling while PeopleTokenRow is a compact count/loading/profile token row.
K4 churn threshold: the shared StagePanel/StageSectionLabel shell is small, and merging would need icon, label, title, body, and optional supporting-text knobs for two live-stage concepts.
K2 domain fork: EventFocusRail is member dashboard event-focus navigation while HostEventToolsCarousel is host management tooling with different data and action contracts.
